name: Software Craft
desc: Comprehensions, exception handling, classes, generators and type hints — writing code others can run.

questions.card: output · for · if — all in one expression.
questions.mission: Uppercase words longer than 2 chars in words into big.
questions.ph: big = [w.upper() for w in words if ...]
questions.sol: big = [w.upper() for w in words if len(w) > 2]

questions.card: Braces build a set of unique results.
questions.mission: Collect the distinct word lengths of words into sizes.

checks.msg: Braces make a set of len(w).

questions.card: Swap keys and values via .items().
questions.mission: Invert d into inv.
questions.ph: inv = {v: k for k, v in d.items()}
questions.sol: inv = {v: k for k, v in d.items()}

questions.card: Two for clauses read outer-to-inner.
questions.mission: Flatten grid into a flat list flat.
questions.ph: flat = [x for row in grid for x in row]
questions.sol: flat = [x for row in grid for x in row]

questions.card: No brackets ⇒ a lazy generator, constant memory.
questions.mission: Sum squares of 1..5 with a generator into total.
questions.ph: total = sum(x * x for x in range(1, 6))
questions.sol: total = sum(x * x for x in range(1, 6))

checks.msg: Drop the brackets — stream it, no list.

questions.card: any() short-circuits at the first True.
questions.mission: Is any number in nums negative? → has_neg.
questions.ph: has_neg = any(x < 0 for x in nums)
questions.sol: has_neg = any(x < 0 for x in nums)

questions.card: all() is True only if every item is.
questions.mission: Are all numbers in nums positive? → all_pos.
questions.ph: all_pos = all(x > 0 for x in nums)
questions.sol: all_pos = all(x > 0 for x in nums)

questions.card: reverse=True flips the order.
questions.mission: Sort nums high-to-low into desc.

questions.card: dict(zip(...)) builds a lookup.
questions.mission: Zip keys with vals into table.

questions.card: Put a if cond else b before the for.
questions.mission: Clamp negatives to 0 in nums into clipped.
questions.ph: clipped = [x if x > 0 else 0 for x in nums]
questions.sol: clipped = [x if x > 0 else 0 for x in nums]

questions.card: Recover from a specific error, never a bare except.
questions.mission: Parse raw to int; on ValueError set n to 0.

questions.card: else runs only when no exception fired.
questions.mission: Set ok False on ValueError, True in the else.
questions.ph: try: ... except ValueError: ok = False ... else: ok = True

questions.card: finally always runs — success or not.
questions.mission: Read into data, then always close() in a finally.

questions.card: raise signals a failure.
questions.mission: Raise a ValueError with message "bad".

questions.card: Subclass Exception for domain-specific errors.
questions.mission: Define AuthError subclassing Exception with a pass body.

questions.card: with open(...) auto-closes the handle.
questions.mission: Read all of "data.txt" into text.
questions.ph: with open("data.txt") as f: text = f.read()
questions.sol:
with open("data.txt") as f:
    text = f.read()

questions.card: Mode "w" opens for writing.
questions.mission: Write "hi" to "out.txt".

questions.card: json.loads string → Python object.
questions.mission: Parse raw into data.

questions.card: json.dumps object → string.
questions.mission: Serialize obj into s.

questions.card: except E as e binds the error object.
questions.mission: Catch any Exception from risky() and store str(e) in err.
questions.ph: try: risky() except Exception as e: err = str(e)

questions.card: __init__ seeds instance state.
questions.mission: Write Point storing x and y.

questions.card: Methods take self and act on its state.
questions.mission: Write Counter with n=0 and an inc() that adds 1.

questions.card: __repr__ controls how the object prints.
questions.mission: Give User a repr of User(name).

questions.card: super().__init__ runs the parent constructor.
questions.mission: Write Admin(User) adding a level, chaining to the parent.

questions.card: @classmethod receives the class as cls.
questions.mission: Add a guest() classmethod to User returning cls("guest").

questions.card: @property exposes a method as an attribute.
questions.mission: Give Circle an area property = 3.14 * r ** 2.

questions.card: __eq__ defines what == means.
questions.mission: Make Money compare by cents.

questions.card: @dataclass auto-writes __init__ and __repr__.
questions.mission: Declare a dataclass Point with x: int, y: int.
questions.ph: @dataclass class Point: x: int / y: int

questions.card: __len__ makes len(obj) work.
questions.mission: Give Bag a length equal to its items.

questions.card: @staticmethod needs neither self nor cls.
questions.mission: Give Math a static add(a, b).

questions.card: yield pauses and streams one value.
questions.mission: Write count_up(n) yielding 0..n-1.
questions.ph: def count_up(n): for i in range(n): yield i

questions.card: Parentheses make a one-shot lazy iterator.
questions.mission: Bind a generator of squares 0..4 to g.
questions.ph: g = (x * x for x in range(5))
questions.sol: g = (x * x for x in range(5))

questions.card: yield from streams a sub-iterable.
questions.mission: Write flatten(rows) yielding every item of every row.
questions.ph: def flatten(rows): for row in rows: yield from row

questions.card: A decorator wraps a function and returns the wrapper.
questions.mission: Write loud(fn) returning a wrapper(*args) that calls fn.
questions.ph: def loud(fn): def wrapper(*args): return fn(*args) / return wrapper

questions.card: @lru_cache caches results by args.
questions.mission: Memoize fib(n) (n<2 → n, else fib(n-1)+fib(n-2)).

questions.card: @wraps(fn) keeps the wrapped name/doc.
questions.mission: Write timed(fn): an inner (*args, **kwargs) wrapper decorated with @wraps(fn).

questions.card: chain concatenates lazily.
questions.mission: Chain a and b into a list joined.

questions.card: next() advances a generator once.
questions.mission: Pull the first value of g into first.

questions.card: A generator keeps memory flat over any size.
questions.mission: Sum 0..999999 without building a list into total.
questions.ph: total = sum(i for i in range(1000000))
questions.sol: total = sum(i for i in range(1000000))

checks.msg: No list — stream it with a generator.

questions.card: Wrap, time, return — the classic profiler.
questions.mission: Write timer(fn): time fn(*args), print the elapsed, return the result.
questions.ph: def timer(fn): def inner(*args): start=... result=fn(*args) print(...) return result / return inner
questions.sol:
def timer(fn):
    def inner(*args):
        start = time.time()
        result = fn(*args)
        print(time.time() - start)
        return result
    return inner

questions.card: Annotate params with name: type and returns with ->.
questions.mission: Write the header for mean taking list[float], returning float.

questions.card: str | None means "maybe a str".
questions.mission: Header for find(key: str) returning str | None.

questions.card: Generics spell out the element type.
questions.mission: Header for evens() returning list[int].

questions.card: dict[str, int] types keys and values.
questions.mission: Header for load(cfg: dict[str, int]) returning None.

questions.card: Fields carry types and optional defaults.
questions.mission: Dataclass Config: host: str, port: int = 8080.
questions.ph: @dataclass class Config: host: str / port: int = 8080

questions.card: Callable types a function argument.
questions.mission: Header for apply(op: Callable, x: int) returning int.
questions.ph: def apply(op: Callable, x: int) -> int:
questions.sol: def apply(op: Callable, x: int) -> int:

questions.card: := assigns inside an expression.
questions.mission: In one condition, bind n = len(data) and test > 0; print n in the body.

questions.card: {x=} prints both the name and its value.
questions.mission: Debug-print x with the = shortcut.

questions.card: match / case is structural dispatch.
questions.mission: Match cmd: "start" → run(), else → stop().
questions.ph: match cmd: case "start": run() / case _: stop()
questions.sol:
match cmd:
    case "start":
        run()
    case _:
        stop()

questions.card: Put it together: a fully typed function body.
questions.mission: Write average(nums: list[float]) -> float returning the mean.
questions.sol:
def average(nums: list[float]) -> float:
    return sum(nums) / len(nums)

questions.card: assert — first line of defense; fails loud in tests.
questions.mission: Assert that add(2, 3) equals 5.

questions.card: Pytest discovers functions named test_*.
questions.mission: Define test_add asserting add(1, 1) == 2.

questions.card: with pytest.raises(E): asserts E was raised.
questions.mission: Assert calling bad() raises ValueError.

questions.card: @pytest.mark.parametrize runs one test many times with different inputs.
questions.mission: Decorate test_sq with two cases: (2, 4), (3, 9).

questions.card: @pytest.fixture — a reusable setup for tests.
questions.mission: Define fixture sample that returns [1, 2, 3].

questions.card: pytest.approx compares floats with tolerance.
questions.mission: Assert result approximates 0.1.

questions.card: MagicMock() stands in for a callable in tests.
questions.mission: Create a mock api using MagicMock().

questions.card: monkeypatch — pytest builtin to patch env/attrs.
questions.mission: Set env var KEY to abc.

questions.card: pytest --cov reports line coverage.
questions.mission: Run pytest with coverage for pkg app.

questions.card: @pytest.mark.skip skips a broken test with a reason.
questions.mission: Skip with reason wip.

questions.card: async def defines a coroutine — call it, get an awaitable.
questions.mission: Define coroutine ping returning 'pong'.

questions.card: await pauses the coroutine until the awaitable finishes.
questions.mission: In tick, await asyncio.sleep(1).

questions.card: asyncio.run(coro) is the entry from sync land.
questions.mission: Kick off main() via asyncio.run.

questions.card: asyncio.gather awaits many coroutines in parallel.
questions.mission: Gather two tasks — a(), b() — into results.

questions.card: asyncio.create_task schedules a coroutine without waiting.
questions.mission: Schedule worker() as t.

questions.card: asyncio.wait_for raises TimeoutError past a deadline.
questions.mission: Bound fetch() to 2s into data.

questions.card: ThreadPoolExecutor — CPU-light I/O parallelism.
questions.mission: Build a pool with 4 workers as pool.

questions.card: Use ProcessPoolExecutor for CPU-bound parallelism (skirts the GIL).
questions.mission: Map work over xs via pool.map.

questions.card: asyncio.Lock serializes access to a resource.
questions.mission: Use lock in a with-style block: async with lock:.

questions.card: An asyncio.Queue gives producers/consumers backpressure.
questions.mission: Create bounded queue size 100 into q.

questions.card: Path composes OS-safe file paths.
questions.mission: Build data/raw.csv as p.

questions.card: json.dumps serializes a dict to a string.
questions.mission: Dump d into raw.

questions.card: re.findall — extract every match.
questions.mission: Pull digits from text into nums.

questions.card: datetime.now() gives current local time.
questions.mission: Capture into ts.

questions.card: Counter — bag-of-things counter with .most_common.
questions.mission: Count chars into tally.

questions.card: defaultdict(list) auto-creates missing values.
questions.mission: Create groups defaulting to list.

questions.card: itertools.chain flattens iterables end-to-end.
questions.mission: Chain a, b into flat.

questions.card: logging.basicConfig(level=...) — one-line logger setup.
questions.mission: Set root level to INFO.

questions.card: parser.add_argument declares a CLI flag.
questions.mission: Add --epochs as int.

questions.card: @contextmanager — build a with-block from a generator.
questions.mission: Decorate timer with @contextmanager.
